title: Define Master Requirements Domain Model (MRL)
body:
Summary
Introduce the foundational domain model for IMQ’s Master Requirements List (MRL). This model will serve as the canonical source of normative role requirements across the platform, independent of user evidence or assessment logic.

🧠 Key Conceptual Distinction (Foundational to IMQ)

| Concept            | Description                                                                 |
|--------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Requirement        | Normative: what a role must/should require                                  |
| Qualification      | Descriptive: what an individual has, based on evidence                      |
| Assessment         | Evaluative: compares individual evidence against requirements               |

🧱 Deliverables (Model Only, No Persistence)

Create a single normalized class: QualificationRequirement

Required Fields

| Field             | Type                   | Notes                                                                |
|------------------|------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| id               | UUID or string         | Unique system identifier                                             |
| name             | string                 | Human-readable requirement name                                      |
| description      | string (optional)      | Brief internal explanation                                           |
| requirementType  | enum                   | One of: Education, Training, Experience, Certification               |
| requirementLevel | enum                   | One of: Required, Preferred, Optional                                |
| source           | string                 | Origin of this requirement (e.g., SOP ID, SME input, audit finding)  |
| approvalStatus   | enum                   | One of: Draft, Approved, Retired                                     |
| approvedBy       | string                 | Who approved the requirement                                         |
| approvedAt       | datetime               | Timestamp of approval                                                |
| lastReviewed     | datetime (optional)    | Informational only                                                   |
| reviewedBy       | string (optional)      | Informational only                                                   |
| version          | string or int          | For future version tracking                                          |
| tags             | list of strings        | Search/filter support (optional)                                     |

Note: Review metadata is informational and does not imply approval status.

🧪 Unit Test Guidance

Decision: Minimal tests included in this issue (Option B).

Unit tests must validate structure only—not behavior, logic, or assessment.

Write tests to confirm:
- You can create a valid QualificationRequirement
- Enums accept only expected values
- Required fields enforce presence

Keep this lean to prevent scope creep.

🚫 Out of Scope

- No persistence
- No UI
- No services
- No user evidence
- No assessment logic
- No mapping to roles

🧭 Outcome

A standalone, strongly typed domain model representing what the organization requires — decoupled from any individual, role, or evidence. This enables:
- Auditability
- Role mapping
- Seed data governance
- Future assessment and AI logic

## Additional Refinements

### ID Field
**Updated Field:**
- `id`: `Guid` — Unique system identifier

**Reasoning:**
- Aligns with .NET conventions and IMQ system patterns
- Avoids ambiguity for Copilot or manual dev work

### Approval and Review Attribution
**Fields Updated:**
- `approvedBy`: `string` — Represented as string for now (user ID or system identifier)
- `reviewedBy`: `string` — Represented as string for now (user ID or system identifier)

**Clarification:**
These are stored as string temporarily. No user entity linkage assumed in this issue.

### Enum Naming Conventions
Ensure enums follow PascalCase naming:
- `RequirementType`
- `RequirementLevel`
- `ApprovalStatus`
